messages.content: You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The harvest that year was very good. We filled every sack and still had grain left over.
Note B: That year's harvest failed completely. We had to borrow from the neighbor just to eat through winter.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "harvest was abundant, surplus grain", "note_b_claim": "harvest failed completely, required borrowing food", "resolution": "flag — complete opposite outcomes for the same harvest year, confirm year, may refer to different seasons or different years"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My parents approved of my marriage from the beginning. They liked my husband's family.
Note B: My parents strongly opposed my marriage. My mother did not speak to me for six months after the wedding.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "parents approved marriage and liked husband's family", "note_b_claim": "parents strongly opposed marriage, mother silent for six months", "resolution": "flag — approval versus strong opposition for same marriage, confirm whether initial opposition softened into approval and which memory refers to which phase"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The fire started in the kitchen of the headman's house and spread from there.
Note B: The fire began in the storehouse at the edge of the village, nowhere near the headman's property.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "fire started in headman's kitchen", "note_b_claim": "fire started in edge-of-village storehouse", "resolution": "flag — origin point of fire is directly contradicted, confirm with multiple witnesses, origin point may affect liability memory"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My husband came home from the Gulf with enough money to build the house and buy land.
Note B: My husband came back from Qatar with almost nothing. The employer had cheated him and he was not paid properly for two years.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "husband returned from Gulf with substantial money, built house and bought land", "note_b_claim": "husband returned with almost nothing, cheated by employer", "resolution": "flag — financial outcome of migration is opposite, may refer to different trips or different speakers, confirm which foreign work period this refers to"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The flood took our lower field entirely. That land is gone, it went into the river.
Note B: The flood touched our field but did not take it. We lost one season of crop but the land is still ours.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "flood permanently destroyed lower field, land lost to river", "note_b_claim": "flood damaged crop only, land remained intact", "resolution": "flag — permanent land loss versus temporary crop loss for same flood event, confirm current ownership of lower field"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father could not read or write. He put his thumbprint on all documents.
Note B: My father kept written accounts of every transaction he ever made. His handwriting was very neat.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "father was illiterate, used thumbprint", "note_b_claim": "father was literate, kept neat written accounts", "resolution": "flag — literacy status is directly contradicted, fundamental personal characteristic, confirm with any surviving documents"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother died at home, in her own bed, surrounded by all of us.
Note B: My mother died in the hospital in the district headquarters. We barely reached her in time.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "mother died at home surrounded by family", "note_b_claim": "mother died in district hospital, family barely arrived in time", "resolution": "flag — place of death is directly contradicted, home versus hospital, high-priority confirmation, emotionally significant distinction"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The new temple was built with donations from the whole community. Every family gave something.
Note B: The temple was built entirely by one wealthy family from the next village. They paid for everything themselves.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "temple funded by community-wide donations", "note_b_claim": "temple funded entirely by one wealthy family", "resolution": "flag — community contribution versus single patron for same construction, confirm from temple records or founding family"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The land dispute with the neighbor family was settled peacefully. We agreed on the boundary and shook hands.
Note B: The land dispute was never resolved. It went to court and we lost. We had to give up that strip of land.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "land dispute settled amicably with handshake", "note_b_claim": "land dispute went to court, speaker's family lost", "resolution": "flag — peaceful resolution versus court loss for same dispute, confirm legal records if available, may involve motivated memory"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My husband was the one who decided we should move to the city. I agreed eventually.
Note B: It was my idea to move. My husband resisted for two years before he came around.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "husband decided to move to city, wife agreed", "note_b_claim": "wife decided to move, husband resisted two years", "resolution": "flag — agency of migration decision is assigned to opposite spouses, confirm from both perspectives if possible"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The school in our village had many students, sometimes fifty in one room.
Note B: Our village school had only twelve or fifteen students when I attended. The teacher knew every child by name.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "school had up to fifty students per room", "note_b_claim": "school had twelve to fifteen students total", "resolution": "flag — enrollment count differs by more than three times, may refer to different eras of the same school, confirm approximate years of attendance"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My grandfather fought in the war and came home a hero. People respected him greatly for it.
Note B: My grandfather avoided the war. He hid in the forest rather than be conscripted. He was not proud of it.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "grandfather fought in war and was respected hero", "note_b_claim": "grandfather evaded conscription, hid in forest, felt shame", "resolution": "flag — direct contradiction of same person's wartime conduct, confirm carefully, family reputation may influence which version is told"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The sel roti at our Tihar celebration was made by my grandmother alone. She let no one help.
Note B: The whole family helped make sel roti. Even the children mixed the batter. It was a group effort every year.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "sel roti made by grandmother alone, no helpers", "note_b_claim": "sel roti made by whole family including children", "resolution": "flag — solitary versus communal preparation for same ritual food, may reflect different years or different speakers' involvement, confirm with other family members"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: We owned the house. My father built it himself with help from the neighbors.
Note B: We were tenants in that house. My father rented it from the family on the hill. We never owned it.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "family owned house, father built it", "note_b_claim": "family rented house, owned by hill family", "resolution": "flag — ownership versus tenancy for same house, fundamental property fact, confirm with any documents or local records"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My eldest son passed his SLC on the first attempt. We were all very proud.
Note B: My eldest failed his SLC the first time and had to repeat the year. He passed on the second try.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "eldest son passed SLC first attempt", "note_b_claim": "eldest son failed first attempt, passed on second", "resolution": "flag — exam outcome directly contradicted, confirm with son or school records, pride-based memory may smooth over a failure"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: There was no rain at all the summer of the drought. The sky was white for months.
Note B: We had some rain that summer but it stopped too early. The crops started well then dried out.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "complete absence of rain throughout summer drought", "note_b_claim": "some early rain occurred before premature stop", "resolution": "flag — total absence versus partial rainfall for same drought period, confirm whether this is the same drought year or two adjacent dry years"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The conflict soldiers never came to our village. We were spared.
Note B: The soldiers came through our village twice. The second time they took grain from three houses.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "conflict soldiers never visited village", "note_b_claim": "soldiers came twice, took grain on second visit", "resolution": "flag — presence versus complete absence of soldiers in same village, sensitive historical memory, confirm with multiple community members"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My parents chose my husband for me. I had not met him before the wedding day.
Note B: I chose my own husband. My parents accepted my choice after some discussion.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "arranged marriage, no prior meeting with husband", "note_b_claim": "love marriage, self-chosen husband accepted by parents", "resolution": "flag — arranged versus self-chosen marriage is directly contradicted, fundamental life event, confirm with wedding ceremony details or family members present"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father never drank. He was very strict about it and forbade it in the house.
Note B: My father kept a clay pot of raksi in the store room. He had a small cup every evening after dinner.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "father was teetotaler, banned alcohol in house", "note_b_claim": "father drank raksi daily in private", "resolution": "flag — complete abstinence versus regular private drinking, may reflect different life periods or one speaker's protected image of father"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The old pipal tree at the village entrance was cut down when the road was widened.
Note B: That pipal tree is still standing. The road went around it. No one would dare cut it.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "pipal tree was cut down for road widening", "note_b_claim": "pipal tree still standing, road routed around it", "resolution": "flag — tree's current existence is directly contradicted, verifiable by site visit, sacred tree status may influence memory"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My uncle returned the loan my father gave him before my father died. The debt was settled.
Note B: My uncle never paid back what he owed my father. It was a source of bitterness until the end.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "family loan repaid before father's death, settled", "note_b_claim": "family loan never repaid, caused lasting bitterness", "resolution": "flag — repaid versus unpaid for same family loan, financially and emotionally significant, may reflect different speakers with different interests in the outcome"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: It was a difficult birth. The child did not breathe at first and everyone was frightened.
Note B: The birth was easy and quick. The child cried immediately and everyone laughed with relief.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "difficult birth, delayed breathing, fear", "note_b_claim": "easy quick birth, immediate cry, relief and laughter", "resolution": "flag — birth difficulty is directly contradicted, confirm whether same birth or two different children's births are being recalled"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The community rebuilt the destroyed houses within one season. People worked together and it was done before winter.
Note B: Some families never rebuilt after the fire. They left the village entirely and the land sat empty for years.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "all destroyed houses rebuilt communally within one season", "note_b_claim": "some families did not rebuild and abandoned the village", "resolution": "flag — complete community recovery versus partial departure, not fully contradictory but significantly different outcomes, confirm how many families rebuilt versus left"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My grandmother was buried according to Hindu custom. There was a full cremation at the ghat.
Note B: My grandmother was Buddhist. Her body was taken to the gompa and the monks performed the rites there.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "grandmother cremated by Hindu rites at ghat", "note_b_claim": "grandmother received Buddhist funeral rites at gompa", "resolution": "flag — religious tradition at death directly contradicted, may reflect mixed-faith family practice or two different grandmothers, confirm which grandmother and her religion"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they state opposite facts about the same event. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father was the one who carried the debt. He borrowed heavily to pay for my wedding.
Note B: My wedding cost almost nothing. We cooked our own food and the family gave what they could. There was no debt.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "factual contradiction", "note_a_claim": "father took on heavy debt to fund wedding", "note_b_claim": "wedding was simple and low-cost, no debt incurred", "resolution": "flag — significant debt versus no debt for same wedding, confirm with family financial history, shame about debt may alter recollection"}
